tests.name: platform_module_present
tests.details: docs/platform-modules/ is empty in project. Framework has docs/platform-modules/mcp-server.md (HYPHEN), but init.sh line 1124 looks for ${PLATFORM}.md i.e. mcp_server.md (UNDERSCORE). Mismatch causes mcp_server platform to never have its module copied.

tests.name: no_residual_backup_dir
tests.details: No .claude-backup directory present

tests.details: PreToolUse Bash matcher includes scripts/pre-commit-gate.sh and CDF pre-commit-checks.sh

tests.name: lint_uat_scenarios_helper_copied
tests.details: scripts/lint-uat-scenarios.sh present (PR #17 U-H fix holds)

tests.name: framework_self_guard_active
tests.details: Running init.sh --non-interactive from $FRAMEWORK with web/typescript/personal exits 1 with [FAIL] Refusing to operate inside the Solo Orchestrator framework repo. PR #18 holds.

framework_bugs.summary: init.sh does not generate .github/workflows/release.yml for personal/mcp_server/light
framework_bugs.reproduction: bash $FRAMEWORK/init.sh --non-interactive --project P --platform mcp_server --track light --deployment personal --language python --project-dir $WORKDIR/P --git-host other --remote-url https://example.com/fake.git --branch-protection-attested --visibility private --allow-existing-dir; ls $WORKDIR/P/.github/workflows/
framework_bugs.expected: Both ci.yml and release.yml present (per Section A test cases ci_workflow_created and release_workflow_created)
framework_bugs.actual: Only ci.yml present; release.yml is missing; release pipeline asserted by Section E (Phase 4) is therefore absent at init time.

framework_bugs.summary: platform-module filename mismatch: init.sh expects mcp_server.md, framework ships mcp-server.md
framework_bugs.reproduction: ls /Users/karl/Documents/Claude Projects/solo-orchestrator/docs/platform-modules/  -> mcp-server.md (hyphen). init.sh line 1124 references ${PLATFORM}.md where PLATFORM=mcp_server -> mcp_server.md (underscore). cp silently no-ops; verify-install warns 'Platform module (mcp_server.md) missing'.
framework_bugs.expected: init.sh copies the mcp_server platform module into docs/platform-modules/ in the generated project; verify-install reports OK.
framework_bugs.actual: docs/platform-modules/ in project is empty; verify-install permanently warns MANUAL: Platform module (mcp_server.md) missing. Naming convention inside the framework (mcp-server.md) does not match the canonical platform key (mcp_server).

documentation_gaps.gap: init.sh --help-non-interactive does not document that mcp_server platform requires the framework to also fix its docs/platform-modules/ filename — users following the docs will silently miss the platform module.

tests.name: start_phase1_exits_zero
tests.details: process-checklist.sh --start-phase1 OK

tests.name: phase1_steps_array_present
tests.details: .phase1_architecture.steps_completed is array

tests.name: each_phase1_step_completes
tests.details: All 5 PHASE1_STEPS (architecture_selected, threat_model_complete, data_model_defined, ui_scaffolding_done, bible_synthesized) completed sequentially

tests.name: phase1_to_phase2_gate_passes
tests.details: scripts/check-phase-gate.sh reports 'Phase gates consistent.' RC=0

tests.name: phase_state_advances_to_2
tests.details: After all phase1 steps complete and check-phase-gate passes, .current_phase remains 0. No script auto-advances. Confirms U-D still open. Manually set current_phase=2 to proceed with upgrade test.

framework_bugs.summary: U-D confirmation: no script advances current_phase after phase 1 gate passes
framework_bugs.reproduction: After completing all 5 phase1_architecture steps and running scripts/check-phase-gate.sh (returns 'Phase gates consistent.', RC=0), inspect .claude/phase-state.json — .current_phase is still 0.
framework_bugs.expected: Some script (check-phase-gate.sh, process-checklist.sh, or a dedicated advance-phase script) should set .current_phase to 2 once gate is consistent and approval is logged.
framework_bugs.actual: Phase remains at 0; users must hand-edit phase-state.json to proceed. (Known-open prior-sweep bug U-D.)

tests.name: upgrade_command_exits_zero
tests.details: intake-wizard.sh --upgrade-deployment organizational completed and printed 'Upgrade Complete'. NOTE: runbook syntax says --upgrade-deployment private_poc, but intake-wizard.sh only accepts personal|organizational; tried that first and it exited with 'Invalid target deployment: private_poc (must be: personal, organizational)'. See documentation_gap.

tests.name: phase_state_preserved
tests.details: .current_phase still 2 and .project still uat-agent-58 after upgrade

tests.name: process_state_preserved
tests.details: .phase1_architecture.steps_completed unchanged; .build_loop history reset (build_loop is per-feature so reset is expected after feature_recorded)

tests.name: build_progress_features_preserved
tests.details: .features_completed still contains 'uat-feat-1' post-upgrade

tests.name: new_required_artifacts_listed
tests.details: Upgrade output lists: 1) Pre-Phase 0 organizational pre-conditions in APPROVAL_LOG.md; 2) PROJECT_INTAKE.md Section 8 (governance pre-flight); 3) assign approval authorities for each phase gate

tests.name: pending_approval_sentinel_respected
tests.details: With .claude/pending-approval.json present, intake-wizard.sh --upgrade-deployment organizational refused with [FAIL] upgrade blocked — pending user decision; phase-state and process-state unchanged. After scripts/pending-approval.sh --resolve, retry succeeded. PR #17 U-F fix holds. (Caveat: blocked exit code is 0, not non-zero — see bug below.)

tests.name: upgrade_changelog_present_in_script
tests.details: scripts/upgrade-project.sh header mentions BL-006 (2026-04-24) and BL-015 (2026-04-25) changelog entries

tests.name: no_template_placeholders_in_output
tests.details: grep -E '__PROJECT_NAME__|__PLATFORM__|__LANGUAGE__|__TRACK__' upgrade.log returned 0 matches

tests.name: deployment_field_written_to_phase_state
tests.details: After successful upgrade, .claude/phase-state.json .deployment is still 'personal'. PROJECT_INTAKE.md was correctly updated to 'Organizational' and CLAUDE.md was updated, and the commit message says 'deployment personal -> organizational', but the canonical deployment field in phase-state.json was never written. Confirms U-J still open.

tests.name: intake_wizard_accepts_runbook_target_value
tests.details: Runbook Section F instructs `intake-wizard.sh --upgrade-deployment {TARGET_DEPLOYMENT}` where TARGET_DEPLOYMENT for private_poc target is 'private_poc' per the runbook's own scenario→deployment mapping. intake-wizard rejects 'private_poc' with 'Invalid target deployment: private_poc (must be: personal, organizational)'. Either the runbook syntax is wrong or the wizard is missing a poc_mode-aware path.

framework_bugs.summary: U-J confirmation: upgrade does not write deployment field to phase-state.json
framework_bugs.reproduction: 1) init personal/mcp_server/light. 2) bash scripts/intake-wizard.sh --upgrade-deployment organizational. 3) jq -r '.deployment' .claude/phase-state.json -> still 'personal'. The upgrade commit (b7a7e8b) only changes .current_phase and adds .last_upgrade; the deployment field is untouched even though the commit message and stdout claim 'personal -> organizational'.
framework_bugs.expected: .claude/phase-state.json .deployment becomes 'organizational' (and .poc_mode set to private_poc when applicable) after the upgrade commits.
framework_bugs.actual: phase-state.json .deployment stays 'personal'; downstream gates and the wizard read this canonical field, so the upgrade is effectively cosmetic. (Known-open prior-sweep bug U-J.)

framework_bugs.summary: intake-wizard --upgrade-deployment has no path to set poc_mode (private_poc / sponsored_poc)
framework_bugs.reproduction: bash scripts/intake-wizard.sh --upgrade-deployment private_poc -> exits with 'Invalid target deployment: private_poc (must be: personal, organizational)'. Even with --upgrade-deployment organizational, .poc_mode in phase-state.json remains null after the upgrade. There is no --upgrade-deployment value or other intake-wizard flag that produces a private_poc state, despite the runbook (and presumably user docs) treating 'upgrade to private_poc' as a first-class operation.
framework_bugs.expected: Either intake-wizard.sh accepts --upgrade-deployment private_poc / sponsored_poc and sets phase-state.json .deployment=organizational + .poc_mode=private_poc/sponsored_poc, or there is a separate documented flag (--gov-mode, --to-private-poc) that does this. Runbook Section F implies the former.
framework_bugs.actual: No supported syntax produces a fresh private_poc state from a personal baseline. organizational is the only accepted value and it leaves poc_mode null.

framework_bugs.summary: Sentinel-blocked upgrade exits 0 instead of non-zero
framework_bugs.reproduction: Write valid .claude/pending-approval.json. Run bash scripts/intake-wizard.sh --upgrade-deployment organizational. Output: [FAIL] upgrade blocked — pending user decision; echo $? -> 0.
framework_bugs.expected: When upgrade refuses to proceed because of a sentinel, the script should exit non-zero so CI/automation can detect the block.
framework_bugs.actual: Exit code 0 despite [FAIL] message; only the human-readable text indicates blocking.

documentation_gaps.gap: Runbook says `intake-wizard.sh --upgrade-deployment {TARGET_DEPLOYMENT}` for private_poc/sponsored_poc, but the script only accepts personal|organizational and has no POC-mode value. Either the runbook syntax or the script's value set is wrong.

Help text for `intake-wizard.sh --upgrade-deployment` describes target as personal|organizational, but does not mention how to upgrade INTO a poc_mode (private_poc, sponsored_poc). User has no documented path.

tests.name: no_uncommitted_secrets
tests.details: grep -lE 'password|api[_-]?key|secret|token' on committed files matches only docstring/template content (security-scan-guide, ci.yml's GITHUB_TOKEN env, framework hooks); no '<key>: <quoted-value>' literal secrets present.

tests.name: log_files_consistent
tests.details: .claude/process-audit.log absent — N/A since no FORCE/reset events occurred during this run.

tests.name: no_orphaned_tempfiles
tests.details: find $WORKDIR/uat-agent-58/.claude -name '*.tmp' returns no results

tests.name: no_framework_contamination
tests.details: git -C $FRAMEWORK rev-parse HEAD = d66e8f276079aa352ba90ac54386e1b1896d7494 (matches captured setup HEAD). git status --short shows only pre-existing untracked .claude/ and Reports/uat-2026-04-26/ which predate this run.

summary: init.sh missed release.yml and platform-module file (mcp_server.md vs mcp-server.md naming mismatch); upgrade flow has no path to private_poc target (intake-wizard rejects the value) and U-J confirmed (deployment field never written to phase-state.json on upgrade); pending-approval sentinel correctly blocks but exits 0; framework not contaminated; PR #17/#18/#19 fixes for U-F, U-H, U-N/U-O, U-A all hold.
